tasks.task: Create File entity in Prisma schema

criterion.title: File entity created in Prisma schema
criterion.details: - File model added with fields: id, domainID, userID, filename, originalName, mimeType, size, s3Key, s3Bucket, createdAt, deletedAt - Indexes on: domainID, userID, mimeType, deletedAt, s3Key - Foreign key: userID → User.id (onDelete: Cascade) - User entity includes `files File[]` relation - Migration generated and applied successfully

criterion.title: POST /files/upload endpoint created
criterion.details: - Route: POST /api/files/upload (multipart/form-data) - Guards: JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard - Permission: FILES.CREATE - Rate limit: 20 requests per hour per user (@Throttle decorator) - Accepts 'files' field (single or multiple, max 10 files)

criterion.title: File validation implemented
criterion.details: - Max file size: 10MB per file - Max files per request: 10 - MIME type whitelist: image/*, application/pdf, office documents, text/*, archives - File extension cross-validated with MIME type - Returns 400 Bad Request with detailed error if validation fails

criterion.title: Filename sanitization implemented
criterion.details: - Special characters removed/replaced with underscores - Path traversal prevented (no ../, ./) - Max filename length: 255 characters - originalName preserved for user reference - filename sanitized for storage

criterion.title: S3 upload logic implemented
criterion.details: - Generates unique S3 key: {domainID}/{userID}/{timestamp}-{filename} - Uploads to S3 with ACL='private' - ContentType set to file's MIME type - Retry logic: 3 attempts with exponential backoff (reuses S3Service from Story 4.1) - Returns 503 Service Unavailable on final S3 failure

criterion.title: File metadata saved to database
criterion.details: - Transaction: All file metadata inserted atomically - On S3 upload success → Insert DB records - On DB insert failure → Cleanup S3 files (compensating transaction) - Returns FileResDto[] with uploaded file metadata

criterion.title: Multiple file upload supported
criterion.details: - Endpoint accepts 1-10 files in single request - Uploads processed sequentially (avoid memory overflow) - Partial success handling: Returns success for uploaded files + errors for failed ones - Response includes array of FileResDto (one per file)

criterion.title: Upload response format validated
criterion.details: - Returns 201 Created on success - Response: { success: true, data: FileResDto[] } - FileResDto excludes: s3Key, s3Bucket, domainID, userID, deletedAt - FileResDto includes: id, filename, originalName, mimeType, size, sizeFormatted, createdAt

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: File Management (AWS S3)
doc.snippet: Complete AC specifications (AC-4.2.1 through AC-4.2.8), file validation rules, S3 upload strategy, database transaction pattern, testing approach

doc.title: Epics Document
doc.snippet: User story, acceptance criteria overview, technical notes on multer, S3 upload, File entity soft-delete pattern

doc.title: Previous Story: AWS S3 Configuration & Service
doc.snippet: S3Service implementation (uploadFile, getPresignedUrl, deleteFile methods), retry logic, AWS configuration pattern, MinIO setup, health check endpoint. **CRITICAL**: Reuse S3Service - DO NOT recreate S3 client or duplicate retry logic.

artifact.reason: Existing S3Service with uploadFile(), getPresignedUrl(), deleteFile() methods. Implements retry logic with exponential backoff (3 attempts). **MUST REUSE** for all S3 operations - inject into FilesService.
S3Service interface contract defining uploadFile, getPresignedUrl, deleteFile, testConnection methods with parameter types and return values
Existing FilesModule with S3Service registered. MUST ADD: FilesService (providers), FilesController (controllers), PrismaModule (imports)
Permission constants structure. FILES permissions ALREADY DEFINED (FILES.CREATE, FILES.VIEW, FILES.DELETE, FILES.VIEW_ALL) - NO NEED TO ADD. Use existing pattern for @Permission decorator.
@Permission decorator usage pattern. Apply as: @Permission('FILES', ActionEnum.CREATE) on upload endpoint
PermissionsGuard implementation. Use with JwtAuthGuard: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard). Enforces permission-based access control via AuthorizationService
@CurrentUser() decorator to extract authenticated user from request. Returns JwtPayload with sub (userID), domainID, phoneNumber, roles. Use to get userID and domainID for file operations
User model with files relation ALREADY ADDED (line 41: files File[]). Verify when creating File entity migration
File entity ALREADY EXISTS in Prisma schema with all required fields and indexes. Migration likely already applied. Verify before attempting to recreate.

constraint.title: File Upload Pattern
constraint.rule: FilesController → FilesService → S3Service (from Story 4.1) + PrismaService
Multipart form-data handling via Multer interceptor: @UseInterceptors(FilesInterceptor('files', 10))
Sequential file processing to avoid memory overflow (don't process all 10 files concurrently)
Validation before upload: Fail-fast approach (validate all files before uploading any)

constraint.title: File Validation Rules
constraint.rule: MIME type whitelist: image/*, application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.*, text/*, application/zip
Cross-validate MIME type with file extension (prevent bypass: .exe disguised as .jpg)
File size limit: 10MB per file (10 * 1024 * 1024 bytes)
File count limit: Max 10 files per request
Filename sanitization: Remove/replace special chars, prevent path traversal (../, ./), max 255 chars

constraint.title: S3 Upload Strategy
constraint.rule: S3 key format: {domainID}/{userID}/{timestamp}-{sanitizedFilename} (ensures uniqueness)
Upload with ACL='private' (no public access)
ContentType set to file's MIME type
**REUSE S3Service from Story 4.1** - retry logic already implemented (3 attempts with exponential backoff)
DO NOT recreate AWS S3 client or duplicate retry logic

constraint.title: Database Transaction Pattern
constraint.rule: Transaction scope: All file metadata inserts (Prisma.file.createMany() in transaction)
On S3 upload success → Insert metadata to DB
On DB insert failure → Compensating transaction: Cleanup S3 files (call S3Service.deleteFile() for each)
Ensures data consistency: File in S3 ⟺ Metadata in DB

constraint.title: Permission-Based Access Control
constraint.rule: FILES.CREATE permission required for upload endpoint
PermissionsGuard enforces permission check (Epic 3 infrastructure)
Multi-tenancy: domainID extracted from JWT (@CurrentUser decorator)
Rate limiting: 20 uploads per hour per user (prevent abuse) using @Throttle decorator

constraint.title: Response Format
constraint.rule: FileResDto excludes sensitive data: s3Key, s3Bucket, domainID, userID, deletedAt
Includes: id, filename, originalName, mimeType, size, sizeFormatted (KB/MB), createdAt
ResponseTransformInterceptor wraps response: { success: true, data: FileResDto[] }

constraint.title: CRITICAL: Do Not Recreate Existing Code
constraint.rule: **File entity ALREADY EXISTS** in prisma/schema.prisma (lines 187-209). DO NOT recreate. Verify migration status first.
**FILES permissions ALREADY DEFINED** in permissions.constant.ts. DO NOT add duplicate constants.
**S3Service ALREADY EXISTS** at src/modules/files/services/s3.service.ts. MUST REUSE - inject into FilesService.
**User.files relation ALREADY EXISTS** in prisma/schema.prisma (line 41). DO NOT add duplicate relation.

interface.name: IS3Service
POST /api/files/upload
interface.signature: Method: POST Content-Type: multipart/form-data Field: 'files' (array, max 10 files) Guards: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard) Permission: @Permission('FILES', ActionEnum.CREATE) Rate Limit: @Throttle({ default: { limit: 20, ttl: 3600000 } }) Request: FormData with 'files' field Response: 201 Created { success: true, data: FileResDto[] } Errors: 400 (validation), 401 (auth), 403 (permission), 413 (size), 429 (rate limit), 503 (S3 failure)

interface.name: FileResDto
@CurrentUser()
@Permission(module, action)

tests.standards: **Testing Framework:** Jest (configured in package.json) **Unit Tests:** Mock external services (S3Service, PrismaService) using jest.mock() **Integration Tests:** Use Supertest for HTTP endpoint testing **E2E Tests:** Use MinIO for S3 testing (avoid AWS costs during development) **Coverage Target:** 90%+ for services, 80%+ for controllers **Test File Naming:** *.spec.ts (unit/integration), *.e2e-spec.ts (E2E) **Mocking Pattern:** Create mock implementations for S3Client, PrismaService in test setup **Test Data:** Use test files of various types (image, PDF, DOCX) and sizes (1KB-10MB) for validation tests
tests.locations: - src/**/*.spec.ts (unit tests co-located with source) - test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts (E2E tests in test/ directory) - src/modules/files/services/files.service.spec.ts (to be created) - src/modules/files/controllers/files.controller.spec.ts (to be created) - test/files-upload.e2e-spec.ts (to be created)

ideas.test: validateFile() with 11MB file should throw BadRequestException
validateFile() with invalid MIME type (application/exe) should throw BadRequestException
validateFile() with valid 1MB image/png should pass validation
sanitizeFilename() with "../../etc/passwd" should return "etc_passwd"
sanitizeFilename() with "my file!@#.pdf" should return "my_file___.pdf"
sanitizeFilename() with 300-char filename should truncate to 255 chars
uploadFiles() should call S3Service.uploadFile() and Prisma.file.createMany()
uploadFiles() with DB insert failure should call S3Service.deleteFile() for cleanup
uploadFiles() with 11 files should throw BadRequestException (count limit)
uploadFiles() with partial S3 success (5/10 fail) should return successful uploads + log errors
POST /files/upload with single file should return 201 Created with FileResDto
POST /files/upload with 10 files should return 201 Created with FileResDto[]
POST /files/upload with 11MB file should return 400 Bad Request
POST /files/upload with invalid MIME type (.exe) should return 400 Bad Request
POST /files/upload without FILES.CREATE permission should return 403 Forbidden
POST /files/upload without JWT should return 401 Unauthorized
POST /files/upload (21st upload in 1 hour) should return 429 Too Many Requests
FileResDto should exclude s3Key, s3Bucket, domainID, userID, deletedAt
FileResDto should include sizeFormatted in KB/MB format
Upload file → Verify in S3 using S3Service.getPresignedUrl()
Upload file → Verify metadata in database using Prisma.file.findUnique()
Simulate DB failure → Verify S3 cleanup (files deleted from S3)
